flashcards.back: A state-space search algorithm that systematically explores candidate solutions by branching into subproblems and pruning branches that cannot yield optimal solutions.

flashcards.front: How is the upper bound calculated in 0/1 Knapsack B&B?
flashcards.back: Using the fractional knapsack relaxation: value of fixed items + fractional value of remaining capacity filled with best remaining items.

flashcards.front: What is the key advantage of B&B over DP for 0/1 Knapsack?
flashcards.back: B&B can be more efficient when the knapsack capacity W is large, as it doesn't depend on W in the same way as DP's O(nW) complexity.

flashcards.front: What does a node in the state space tree represent?
flashcards.back: A partial solution where decisions have been made about some items (included or excluded) and remaining items are yet to be decided.

flashcards.front: Why are items sorted by value-to-weight ratio?
flashcards.back: To maximize the effectiveness of the fractional knapsack bound calculation, ensuring we get the tightest possible upper bounds.
